questions.text: Who proposed the Five Kingdom Classification?
questions.explanation: Whittaker proposed it in 1969 based on cell structure, body organization, mode of nutrition, etc.

questions.text: The five kingdoms are Monera, Protista, Fungi, Plantae and:
questions.explanation: Animalia is the fifth kingdom.

questions.text: The only kingdom that consists of prokaryotic organisms is:
questions.explanation: Bacteria are the sole members of Kingdom Monera.

questions.text: Bacteria that live in extreme salty areas are called:
questions.explanation: Archaebacteria differ from other bacteria in having a different cell wall structure.

questions.text: Cyanobacteria are also known as:
questions.explanation: They are photosynthetic autotrophs similar to green plants.

questions.text: The smallest living cells known that can survive without oxygen are:
questions.explanation: Mycoplasma lack a cell wall and are pathogenic in animals and plants.

questions.text: Kingdom Protista includes:
questions.explanation: Boundaries of this kingdom are not well defined.

questions.text: Diatoms belong to which group of Protista?
questions.explanation: Diatoms have silica-embedded cell walls forming 'Diatomaceous earth'.

questions.text: Which of the following is responsible for 'Red Tides' in the sea?
questions.explanation: Gonyaulax is a dinoflagellate that undergoes rapid multiplication.

questions.text: Euglenoids have a protein-rich layer instead of a cell wall called:
questions.explanation: Pellicle makes their body flexible.

questions.text: Slime moulds are ________ protists.
questions.explanation: They move along decaying twigs and leaves engulfing organic matter.

questions.text: The causative agent of Malaria (Plasmodium) is a:
questions.explanation: Sporozoans have an infectious spore-like stage in their life cycle.

questions.text: The cell wall of fungi is composed of:
questions.explanation: Chitin is a defining feature of fungi.

questions.text: A symbiotic association between algae and fungi is:
questions.explanation: Algae prepare food and fungi provide shelter and absorb nutrients.

questions.text: Which fungus is used in the production of bread and beer?
questions.explanation: Saccharomyces cerevisiae is a unicellular fungus.

questions.text: Penicillium belongs to which class of fungi?
questions.explanation: Ascomycetes are also known as sac-fungi.

questions.text: Mushrooms, bracket fungi or puffballs belong to:
questions.explanation: Commonly known as club fungi.

questions.text: Fungi Imperfecti is the name given to:
questions.explanation: Only their asexual or vegetative phases are known.

questions.text: Who discovered the virus and gave the name 'contagium vivum fluidum'?
questions.explanation: M.W. Beijerinck demonstrated that extract of infected tobacco plants could infect healthy ones.

questions.text: Viruses are not included in any kingdom because they are:
questions.explanation: They are considered on the borderline of living and non-living.

questions.text: The protein coat of a virus is called:
questions.explanation: Capsid is made of small subunits called capsomeres.

questions.text: Viroids differ from viruses in having:
questions.explanation: T.O. Diener discovered viroids; they are smaller than viruses.

questions.text: Prions are infectious agents made of:
questions.explanation: They cause diseases like Mad Cow Disease and CJD.

questions.text: Which organism is a very good indicator of air pollution?
questions.explanation: Lichens do not grow in polluted areas, especially those with SO₂.

questions.text: In bacteria, DNA is ________ and ________.
questions.explanation: Prokaryotic DNA is usually a single circular chromosome.

questions.text: Methanogens are present in the gut of:
questions.explanation: They produce methane (biogas) from the dung of these animals.

questions.text: Heterocysts are specialized cells found in some Cyanobacteria for:
questions.explanation: Examples include Nostoc and Anabaena.

questions.text: Sleeping sickness is caused by:
questions.explanation: It is a flagellated protozoan.

questions.text: The reserve food material in fungi is:
questions.explanation: Similar to animals, fungi store energy as glycogen.

questions.text: Black rust of wheat is caused by:
questions.explanation: Puccinia graminis tritici.

questions.text: Kingdom Monera members are grouped under four shapes: Coccus, Bacillus, Vibrium and:
questions.explanation: Spherical, Rod-shaped, Comma-shaped, and Spiral.

questions.text: Which bacteria help in making curd from milk?
questions.explanation: They convert lactose sugar into lactic acid.

questions.text: The cell wall of plants is mainly composed of:
questions.explanation: Kingdom Plantae members are eukaryotic chlorophyll-containing organisms.

questions.text: Bladderwort and Venus fly trap are examples of:
questions.explanation: They are partially heterotrophic.

questions.text: Sexual reproduction in fungi involves three steps: Plasmogamy, Karyogamy and:
questions.explanation: Meiosis in zygote results in haploid spores.

questions.text: The algal component of lichen is called ________ and fungal is ________.
questions.explanation: Algae is phycobiont (autotrophic), fungi is mycobiont (heterotrophic).

questions.text: Tobacco Mosaic Virus (TMV) has ________ as genetic material.
questions.explanation: Most plant viruses have ssRNA.

questions.text: Bacteriophages (viruses infecting bacteria) usually have:
questions.explanation: They usually have double stranded DNA.

questions.text: Cuscuta is a ________ plant.
questions.explanation: It is a total stem parasite.

questions.text: The Kingdom Protista forms a link with which other kingdoms?
questions.explanation: Protista contains the ancestors of multicellular eukaryotes.
